scope.summary:
Goal: implement a single app-wide SharedInteractionBus — the substrate that lets all editor panes (code, rich-text, graph, canvas) share one selection model, one clipboard, and one command registry. The expected diff shape is: (a) one new Rust module src/frontend/shared/interaction_bus.rs (~350–500 lines) defining the bus types and an egui-integrated singleton, (b) thin adapter shims in each existing pane module (src/frontend/panes/code_editor.rs, rich_text_editor.rs, graph_pane.rs, canvas_pane.rs) that register into the bus rather than own ad-hoc clipboard/command state, (c) a kittest integration test src/frontend/tests/interaction_bus_tests.rs proving cross-pane selection propagation and clipboard round-trip.

Files to CREATE:
  - src/frontend/shared/interaction_bus.rs  — defines SharedSelection, ClipboardPayload, CommandBus, CommandDescriptor, CommandHandler; holds an Arc<Mutex<InteractionBus>> accessible through egui's app data storage via Context::data_mut.

Files to EDIT:
  - src/frontend/shared/mod.rs  — add `pub mod interaction_bus;`
  - src/frontend/panes/code_editor.rs  — replace per-pane clipboard + keybinding dispatch with bus calls; call bus.clipboard_write / bus.clipboard_read; register editor commands (Copy, Cut, Paste, SelectAll, Find) via bus.register_command.
  - src/frontend/panes/rich_text_editor.rs  — same pattern.
  - src/frontend/panes/graph_pane.rs  — register node-selection into SharedSelection; clipboard writes (copy node ref as loom:// URI).
  - src/frontend/panes/canvas_pane.rs  — same as graph_pane; canvas node selection feeds SharedSelection.

Step-by-step implementation notes:
  1. Define SharedSelection as an enum: None | TextRange { pane_id: PaneId, start: usize, end: usize, text: String } | BlockRef { pane_id: PaneId, block_id: String } | NodeRef { pane_id: PaneId, node_id: String }. Pane that holds focus owns the active variant; others observe.
  2. Define ClipboardPayload as an enum: PlainText(String) | LoomBlockRef(String) | AtelierRef(String) | RichContent { mime: String, bytes: Vec<u8> }. Write through arboard (add to Cargo.toml if not present; it is pure Rust, MIT licensed). On read, return the richest available variant the pane can consume.
  3. Define CommandDescriptor { id: &'static str, label: String, keybind: Option<egui::KeyboardShortcut>, handler: CommandHandler } where CommandHandler = Arc<dyn Fn(&mut egui::Ui, &mut InteractionBus) + Send + Sync>. CommandBus is Vec<CommandDescriptor>.
  4. CommandPalette (ported from React CommandPalette.tsx) drives the command bus: collect all registered descriptors, display in a fuzzy-searchable egui modal with AccessKit id "command-palette-list" + role List; on confirm call handler.
  5. Store the InteractionBus in egui Context app data: ctx.data_mut(|d| d.insert_temp(egui::Id::new("handshake_interaction_bus"), bus.clone())). Every pane retrieves it the same way.
  6. Keybind dispatch: each pane, in its update() call, calls bus.dispatch_keybind(ctx, ui) after its own event handling. The bus iterates registered descriptors and checks egui::InputState::consume_shortcut.
  7. AccessKit: register the command palette trigger button with AccessKit author_id="command-palette-trigger", role=Button, actions=[Press]. Register the search input with author_id="command-palette-search", role=TextField. Each command list item uses author_id="cmd-{descriptor.id}", role=ListItem.
  8. Add a pane_focus_changed notification: when a pane receives click focus, it calls bus.set_focus_owner(pane_id) so SharedSelection.pane_id tracks which pane currently owns selection.
  9. Cross-pane Copy: when the user presses Ctrl+C in any pane, that pane builds a ClipboardPayload and calls bus.clipboard_write(payload). The bus calls arboard::Clipboard::set_text for PlainText, and also caches the richest variant in-memory for same-session cross-pane Paste.
  10. Tests: src/frontend/tests/interaction_bus_tests.rs — (a) unit: build a bus, register two panes, simulate selection change in pane A, read SharedSelection and assert it reflects pane A; (b) kittest harness: open code pane, select text, Ctrl+C, focus rich-text pane, Ctrl+V, assert pasted text equals source; (c) command bus: register a test command, call dispatch_command by id, assert handler invoked.

Dependencies / unblock conditions:
  - MT-001 (code editor pane exists in src/frontend/panes/code_editor.rs) must be merged first.
  - MT-011 (rich-text editor pane) must be merged.
  - MT-021 (graph pane) must be merged.
  - arboard crate (^3.x, pure Rust, MIT) must be added to Cargo.toml if not already present.

Risk if missed: every downstream E5 MT (MT-032 through MT-036) requires the bus as a common substrate. Without it, each subsequent MT would build its own ad-hoc selection/clipboard, fragmenting the 'melt-together' invariant and making the parity proof suite impossible.
acceptance_criteria: SharedSelection propagates: selecting text in the code pane and reading SharedSelection from any other pane returns the correct TextRange with matching text and pane_id — proven by kittest harness.

Clipboard round-trip: Ctrl+C in code pane then Ctrl+V in rich-text pane pastes the identical text — proven by kittest harness; arboard::Clipboard also receives the write (verified by reading system clipboard in the test).

Command palette modal opens with Ctrl+Shift+P (or OS-appropriate shortcut), lists all registered commands, filters by fuzzy query, and fires the selected command handler — proven by kittest interaction test.

All four pane types (code, rich-text, graph, canvas) register at least one command into the bus and at least one clipboard write path — proven by grep of bus.register_command and bus.clipboard_write call sites.
AccessKit nodes present: 'command-palette-trigger' (Button), 'command-palette-search' (TextField), and at least one 'cmd-{id}' (ListItem) — proven by AccessKit tree dump in kittest.
cargo test --package handshake_native_frontend passes with no failures across all interaction_bus_tests.
No per-pane ad-hoc clipboard state or separate keybind dispatch tables remain after the refactor (old ad-hoc code removed, not duplicated alongside).
proof_targets: cargo test -p handshake_native_frontend interaction_bus -- --nocapture  =>  all tests PASS, no panics
kittest harness: simulate Ctrl+C in code pane, Ctrl+V in rich-text pane, assert pasted text equals source selection
kittest harness: open command palette via Ctrl+Shift+P, type partial command name, assert filtered list, press Enter, assert handler side-effect
AccessKit tree snapshot: assert nodes 'command-palette-trigger', 'command-palette-search', 'cmd-Copy' present with correct roles
implementation_notes: Use egui::Id::new("handshake_interaction_bus") as the stable key for ctx.data_mut storage; every pane retrieves the bus by this id in its update() fn.
arboard ^3 is cross-platform (Windows/macOS/Linux) pure Rust; add under [dependencies] in Cargo.toml.
CommandDescriptor.keybind uses egui::KeyboardShortcut { modifiers, key }; standard VS Code bindings: Copy=Ctrl+C, Cut=Ctrl+X, Paste=Ctrl+V, SelectAll=Ctrl+A, Find=Ctrl+F, CommandPalette=Ctrl+Shift+P.

The egui modal for the CommandPalette: use egui::Window::new("Command Palette").anchor(egui::Align2::CENTER_TOP, [0.0, 48.0]).show(ctx, |ui| { ... }); open only when bus.command_palette_open flag is true.
Pane focus: each pane's update() fn calls bus.set_focus_owner(self.pane_id) only when ui.memory(|m| m.has_focus(self.pane_egui_id)) is true, avoiding spurious resets.
React reference: CommandPalette.tsx defines CommandPaletteAction { id, label, description, keywords, disabled, stableId }; match this shape in CommandDescriptor; stableId maps to AccessKit author_id.
ports_from_react: app/src/components/CommandPalette.tsx — CommandPaletteAction shape, fuzzy filter logic (matchesQuery), keyboard nav (ArrowUp/Down/Enter/Escape), overlay modal pattern
binds_backend_api: None — this MT is pure frontend bus; clipboard write uses arboard (OS clipboard); no backend API calls required

risks: Arc<Mutex<InteractionBus>> contention: multiple panes calling bus methods in the same egui frame could deadlock if a handler re-enters the lock.
arboard fails on headless CI (no clipboard daemon on Linux); tests that assert system clipboard content will hang or fail.
Keybind conflicts: registering Ctrl+C in the bus conflicts with egui's default text-widget copy if panes do not suppress the default handler.
Stale pane_id after a pane is closed: SharedSelection may reference a closed pane; consumers must guard against dangling pane_id.
AccessKit author_id collisions if two command palette instances are ever rendered (e.g. in kittest harness with multiple windows).
minimum_controls: Use a try_lock pattern in update() paths and log a warning on contention; never block the egui frame thread.
Gate arboard system-clipboard assertions behind a #[cfg(not(ci))] feature flag or check for DISPLAY env var; fallback to in-memory clipboard assertion only in CI.
Each pane calls ui.input_mut(|i| i.consume_shortcut(&shortcut)) before bus.dispatch_keybind to suppress egui default handling.
SharedSelection read must check pane_id exists in the live pane registry before use; return None if pane is gone.
Ensure the egui::Id for the command palette modal is scoped to the window id so multi-window harness tests do not collide.
